subject: mathematics
description: Observe and monitor students as they identify 2D shapes and 3D objects in pictures or in real-world context

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student identify shapes in real-world objects, such as in a classroom, playground, or home?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student name the shapes they see correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student identify the characteristics of each shape (number of sides, corners, curves, etc.)?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student identify shapes from different perspectives (e.g., recognizing a triangle when it is upside down)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the 2D shapes correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student sort the 2D shapes into the correct category (circle, triangle, square, rectangle)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Does the student understand the concept of flatness in 2D shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the 3D objects correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student sort the 3D objects into the correct category (sphere, cube, cone, cylinder)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Does the student understand the concept of depth in 3D objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the number of faces, edges, and vertices of each 3D object?

subject: mathematics
description: Conduct interviews with students to assess their understanding of shapes and space. Ask open-ended questions and encourage students to use descriptive language and gestures

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you tell me the names of some 2D shapes that you know?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you show me a 2D shape using your hands or fingers? What is it called?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: What is the difference between a circle and a square? Can you show me with your hands or draw it on paper?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you tell me the names of some 3D shapes that you know?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you show me a 3D shape using toys or objects? What is it called?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: How do 3D shapes differ from 2D shapes? Can you show me with your hands or draw it on paper?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you describe a real-life object that is shaped like a cylinder?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you tell me what a cube looks like and give me an example of a real-life object shaped like a cube?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: How do shapes and space relate to each other? Can you give me an example of how shapes are used in our daily lives?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample question: Can you think of a fun activity we can do to learn more about shapes and space?

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students using a checklist as they find shapes or paths hidden in a picture or space

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist item: Can the student locate an object in a simple picture?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist item: Can the student complete a maze?

subject: mathematics
description: Question students about shapes or paths hidden in a picture or space

subject: mathematics
description: Check to see whether learners can use manipulatives to count and build quantities up to 20

subject: mathematics
description: Observe learners write the numeral in figures and words after being shown a representation of a particular quantity up to 20

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to use manipulatives, interactive games and number lines to identify errors in a counting sequence up to 20

subject: mathematics
description: Observe learners as they match pictorial representation of quantities with the correct number up to 20

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to draw quantities to represent a given amount up to 20

subject: mathematics
description: Check as learners use vertical and horizontal number lines to count forward to 100 by 1s, 2s, 5s and 10s and forward and backwards from 20 by 1s, 2s, 5s and 10

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to learners as they call out numerals based on a representation shown of a particular quantity up to 20

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to learners as they count in sequence to 100

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners select a card of their choice then pair with each other to match the symbolic representation of the quantity shown on their card

subject: mathematics
description: Learners form a circle and take turns to skip count

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to come up with their own sequence up to 20 and explain to the class how they formed that sequence

subject: mathematics
description: In groups, allow learners to form sets of 2s and 5s with objects given (up to 20). Learners will then count the number of groups made in each case

subject: mathematics
description: Using manipulatives, allow learners to form sets up to 20 and match numerals for each set

subject: mathematics
description: As groups, learners fish for numerals up to 20 and draw quantities to represent the numeral

subject: mathematics
description: Ask learners to draw a picture showing the number of cupcakes in 4 packages if there are three cupcakes in each package

subject: mathematics
description: Using given sets of manipulatives, allow learners to count and state the final number for each set

subject: mathematics
description: Are students able to recite number sequences beginning with 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7… etc.?

subject: mathematics
description: Are students able to count using one to one correspondence?

subject: mathematics
description: Can students identify the number that comes after or before a number from 1-20?

subject: mathematics
description: Are students able to skip count in 2's, 5's and 10's on a number line?

subject: mathematics
description: Can students recognize 0 as the absence of items in a set? As a starting point for counting on the number line?

subject: mathematics
description: Observe learners as they work with concrete materials. Are they able to represent numbers accurately?

subject: mathematics
description: Check to see if learners are making use of resources correctly

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they make pictorial representation of numbers. Have them explain representations

subject: mathematics
description: Count the stars, then colour the same amount in the ten frames

subject: mathematics
description: Students will match the number card to the ten-frame representation

subject: mathematics
description: Observe as they find examples of given numbers in the environment

subject: mathematics
description: Guide students as they label number line or put the numbers in order on a chart

subject: mathematics
description: Have students place numbers on a number line with benchmarks

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to them as they convince their partner that they have placed the numbers on the line correctly

subject: mathematics
description: Have students use two different objects to create a model for a given number

subject: mathematics
description: Have them create a poster using art material or something of that nature

subject: mathematics
description: Subitizing Rings: Write the numerals on each end of the clothespins, and then encourage students to match the numeral on the pin to the matching amount of dots on each of the Subitizing Rings

subject: mathematics
description: Challenge them by timing how fast they can match the pins to their correct spots on the ring

subject: mathematics
description: Check to see whether learners can count manipulatives in two given groups and identify the group with more or the one with fewer

subject: mathematics
description: Students are asked to make predictions about 2D shapes and 3D objects in picture or in real-world context

subject: mathematics
description: Students will be placed into groups of 3. Two will dip a number and jump on the number line based on their chosen numbers. The distance (number of squares) jumped by each will be observed and recorded by the 3rd group member

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they count given quantities and identify the larger and smaller group

subject: mathematics
description: Have students count and write numerals based on a representation shown for groups to identify the one that has less or more (students may be given groups with less first or more)

subject: mathematics
description: In the given columns students will draw one more or less object of a given set

subject: mathematics
description: Several pictures of different quantities will be presented. Students should look at pictures, count the amount, order them and provide the ordinal position of the picture

subject: mathematics
description: In groups, students will be given two numbers and be asked to create a set that comes between the numbers. Each group will share their set with the other groups

subject: mathematics
description: List the ordinal positions going forward as well as in reverse order

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners pair with each other to arrange different given coloured objects (e.g., marbles, small balls, eggs etc.) based on assigned ordinal positions

subject: mathematics
description: Have students work in pairs and choose and write in symbols a number from eleven to nineteen and take that many sticks. Have them count out and make one bundle of ten using the hair tie, then write and draw what they have

subject: mathematics
description: Ask the pairs to return to the whole group with their drawings, keeping them hidden. Have the pairs take turns to describe to the class what they have drawn, and ask classmates to say what number it is. The drawing done by the pair will then be shown

subject: mathematics
description: Match ordinal numbers to their numerals

subject: mathematics
description: Write the number that is formed based on ten plus some

subject: mathematics
description: Use of self-assessment tools and checklist for observable behaviors from students (e.g., students' attitude while they continue to work in groups and the extent to which students are able to complete tasks)

subject: mathematics
description: Assess ability to identify and recognize quantities in teens using tens and ones in concrete, pictorial and symbolic forms

subject: mathematics
description: Allow children to count teen numbers using tens and ones

subject: mathematics
description: Introduce The Number Slide. The Number Slide transforms the expanded form numbers (e.g., 10+1, 10+2, etc.) into the conventional number (e.g., 11, 12, etc.)

subject: mathematics
description: In small groups students will be given unit cubes to make stacks of tens which will be traded for a single base ten rod

subject: mathematics
description: Given other manipulatives such as shells, bottle tops, candies etc. and two plastic glasses of different sizes, students will place only ten in the larger cup and the remainder in the smaller cup and record their findings

subject: mathematics
description: Learners will be given a group of manipulatives less than 20. Each learner will count the amount given and record it and expand the given amount to represent the amount of tens and ones. The learner will share their findings with the class

subject: mathematics
description: Given a worksheet to complete, learners will circle groups of tens and count the remaining as ones

subject: mathematics
description: Roll, Build and Write: Allow students the fun of rolling dice, along with the opportunity to practice building a number with tens and ones

subject: mathematics
description: I Have / Who Has: This game is played with an I Have / Who Has deck. Each student gets one card. The person with "start" begins by reading their card. All cards follow the pattern, "I have_____. Who has___?" So a student with 1 tens and 4 ones on their card would say, "I have 14, who has 18?" Then the learner with one ten and eight ones would read their card. Play continues until all students have had a turn or you reach the card that says end.

subject: mathematics
description: Several pictures depicting different quantities are presented: Look at the picture, count the amount and represent it in a mathematical equation

subject: mathematics
description: Generate simple mathematical equations using base ten materials to represent teen numbers (e.g., 16 = 10+6)

subject: mathematics
description: Assess predictions about 2D shapes undergoing a transformation. Example: "Which 2D shape will look the same if it is turned upside down?"

subject: mathematics
description: Ask students questions about shapes, objects, and space to assess their understanding of the concepts

subject: mathematics
description: Students are required to identify and name shapes with different size, colour, orientation, as well as when they undergo transformation

subject: mathematics
description: Example question: "Can you identify examples of triangular shapes in the classroom?"

subject: mathematics
description: Assess students' ability to match or draw quantities up to 10

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic check: Can students make multiple representations from a story problem structure?

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners use manipulatives to subtract

subject: mathematics
description: Give students 10 buttons and make them take away different quantities from it

subject: mathematics
description: Check that learners can work out the different number combinations that add up to 10

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to place manipulatives on a number line to represent the story being told

subject: mathematics
description: Learners are introduced using a diagram or picture to communicate an addition equation

subject: mathematics
description: Introduce learners to the idea of recording what the diagram shows by allowing them to write the number sentence

subject: mathematics
description: Check to see whether students can use manipulatives to count and build quantities up to 100. For example, student can use coins to assist in the process. Observation Checklist Students can oCount forward by 1s over the decade oCount backward by 1s over the decadeProvide opportunities for students to experience counting in 1s, 2s, 5s and 10s, to 100 in engaging in real life situations. For example, through games, songs (examples can be found https://www.canteach.ca/

subject: mathematics
description: Can students recognize that a variable may be represented by shapes or objects when finding unknown quantity? Factor Entrance Slip Identify and call out the items in the bowl: 2 red apples, a green apple, one banana Retrieved from https://images.google.com/

subject: mathematics
description: Have students use a number line to count back to subtract

subject: mathematics
description: Check that students can write equations to represent their work

subject: mathematics
description: Check that learners can represent the difference between two numbers using concrete materials and diagrams

subject: mathematics
description: Students will create their own number sentences using manipulatives and tens frame provided and share them with the class

subject: mathematics
description: Check that learners can work in pairs to link story problems to the corresponding expression

subject: mathematics
description: Pairs share with the class and explain why they chose their answer

subject: mathematics
description: Using an egg carton with a given amount, students will find out how many are needed to fill it, then write the number sentence

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to work in pairs using number grids to answer questions on subtraction by counting back

subject: mathematics
description: Make copies of the tens frames and allow students to complete addition equations for stories given

subject: mathematics
description: Number bonds to 10: Learners explore the different number combinations that add up to make 10

subject: mathematics
description: Check that learners can work out the different number combinations that add up to 10

subject: mathematics
description: Use caution tape to model a bus with 16 spaces. Probe students by asking: "How many passengers can the bus hold?" and "How do you know?"

subject: mathematics
description: Place students in the taped area and ask: "How many passengers are on the bus?" and "The bus needs to be filled. How many more passengers do we need?"

subject: mathematics
description: How many blue counters are there? (Write 5) And how many red counters are there? (Write 3) 5 and 3 make how many counters? Record the equation 5 + 3 = 8

subject: mathematics
description: Present story: "Grade 1 students are baking cupcakes. Yesterday, they baked 4 cupcakes. Today they baked another 3. How many cupcakes did Grade 1 bake?" Observe as students model with materials

subject: mathematics
description: Have students use manipulatives/pictures to make, draw and/or tell the number amount in a group or set

subject: mathematics
description: Check to see that learners can build groups using manipulatives

subject: mathematics
description: Have students write symbols (numbers) to represent an amount in a group/set

subject: mathematics
description: Have students write/transfer manipulatives/pictorial addition amounts to symbolic representation

subject: mathematics
description: Class discussion on the symbol (+) of addition. Teacher demonstrates/prompts as students come up with their own words that tell (+) addition

subject: mathematics
description: In small groups, students are given cards or manipulatives to write the symbolic representation of what is on the card or what they made using the manipulatives

subject: mathematics
description: In pairs, small groups or whole class: students roll two dice, then write symbolic representations showing additive thinking

subject: mathematics
description: Make predictions as to what may come next in a given pattern. Skills

subject: mathematics
description: Create and extend simple patterns with 3 -5 elements.

subject: mathematics
description: Create patterns resulting from repeating an operation or making a repeated change to an attribute.

subject: mathematics
description: Copy and extend a repeating pattern (using manipulatives, diagrams, body movements/actions/sounds)

subject: mathematics
description: Translate simple repeating patterns (E.g., a repeating orientation pattern to a repeating sound pattern, etc.).

subject: mathematics
description: Copy and extend growing patterns.

subject: mathematics
description: Recognize patterns as either repeating or growing. Present learners with a piece of fabric with a similar design as shown below. Learners will look at the illustration to identify the rule and add on to the repetition. Checklist Can identify shapes and rule (described in own words) Can accurately continue the pattern ) ) Retrieved from https://images.google.com/

subject: mathematics
description: Can students demonstrate patterns through dancing, or performing actions? E.g. clap, stomp feet, click. Observation Have students listen to a sound pattern based on the following. Students will line up to continue the pattern by demonstrating the next correct action (term). Retrieved from https://images.google.com/

subject: mathematics
description: Provide opportunities for students to build, extend and describe patterns formed with letters, numbers and shapes. Retrieved from https://images.google.com/

subject: mathematics
description: Provide opportunities for learners to create their pattern and explain the rule formed by these patterns. Grade 2 Mathematics Curriculum 85 Specific Curriculum Outcomes Inclusive Assessment Strategies Inclusive Learning Strategies Values:

subject: mathematics
description: Acknowledge patterns in their environment by identifying and describing the pattern observed around them.

subject: mathematics
description: Identify and enjoy patterns in poems, stories, (folk and modern) within their culture. Student can describe pattern Student demonstrates next term in pattern (e.g. two claps, a snap and a tap) Retrieved from https://images.google.com/

subject: mathematics
description: Are students able to describe patterns given and patterns formed? E.g. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ___, ____ Concrete

subject: language-arts
description: to transition between subjects

subject: language-arts
description: to provide a short o pportunity to stretch and/or relax

subject: language-arts
description: to connect a well know song to the lesson's focus After the listening experience, engage the class or small groups of learners about:

subject: language-arts
description: was the selection enjoyable and why?

subject: language-arts
description: how did the selection make them feel?

subject: language-arts
description: what was a s pecial part of the selection they would like to share?

subject: language-arts
description: what other songs, stories, etc. would they enjoy listening to? As learners respond, make note of their participation, language choice, vocabulary use, sentence use, etc. The Sounds of Silence (SCO 1. 2): Gather learners together and demonstrate how to pause and listen by asking the learners to create total silence in the classroom. Once learners are silent, direct their attention to the remaining ambient or "non voice" sounds . As a sound is identified , engage the class in a "whisper" conversation to discuss the messages and meaning each sound convey s. For example:

subject: language-arts
description: truck passing by – where do you

subject: language-arts
description: As each Treasure H unt unfolds, the teacher observes, asks questions and provides feedback.

subject: language-arts
description: How do you know this is a list?

subject: language-arts
description: Why is this list important?

subject: language-arts
description: How do you know this is a story? Who do you

subject: mathematics
description: Write the number (symbol) to the set/group shown

subject: mathematics
description: Use symbols to represent the pictures shown

subject: mathematics
description: Match the pictorial representation to its symbolic representation

subject: mathematics
description: Draw any amount of objects in the circles then write the correct number symbol to match

subject: mathematics
description: Given a working card with numbers greater than 20 (e.g., show: 23 + 17 =)

subject: mathematics
description: Match the pictorial representation to its symbolic representation

subject: mathematics
description: Students complete self-assessment after symbolic representation tasks

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to learners describe equal collections of objects as "___ groups of ___"

subject: mathematics
description: Check to see that learners can build groups (1s-10s) using manipulatives

subject: mathematics
description: Learners can form groups using 20 objects (advanced learners can form groups exceeding 20 objects)

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners represent their work by writing repeated addition equations

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners count groups of objects on a worksheet to find a total

subject: mathematics
description: Check that learners can share items into equal groups by circling and then counting the number of groups made

subject: mathematics
description: Check that learners can build a group of numbers in rows and columns using an array

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners create different groups, as a class, in the form of a game (musical chairs style). Teacher rolls die, calls number, students form groups before music ends

subject: mathematics
description: At end of each grouping: count students in group, number of groups, and total

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to discover the different ways in which an even number of items/manipulatives can be shared equally. Example: 20 items can be shared in 2s, 4s, 5s and 10s

subject: mathematics
description: Students fill out cards for each equal group they make

subject: mathematics
description: Can be given at the beginning of the topic as well as the end. Teacher can compare pre/post to see whether learners have made progress

subject: mathematics
description: Look at pictures, count groups, write repeated addition equation

subject: mathematics
description: Skip count to find totals

subject: mathematics
description: Circle groups of items equally and write total number of groups

subject: mathematics
description: Count the number of items represented in pictures

subject: mathematics
description: Skip count to find the total of each grouping shown

subject: mathematics
description: Circle groups of items equally then write the total number of groups in each case

subject: mathematics
description: Write mathematical statements to describe what is shown (e.g., "2 bears shared into 2s is 1 group")

subject: mathematics
description: In small groups, learners use objects to form arrays. Example: Make 3 groups of 4

subject: mathematics
description: Skip count the total number of objects

subject: mathematics
description: Write a mathematical statement to represent the array (4+4+4=12, "three groups of 4 make 12")

subject: mathematics
description: Place learners in pairs with even number of items. Allow pairs to discover how many ways items can be shared equally

subject: mathematics
description: Fill out cards with columns: Items, Groups of, Number of Groups, Total (e.g., 20 items in 2s = 10 groups = 20 total)

subject: mathematics
description: Fair-sharing problems: "John has 20 apples. He wants to give equal shares to his 4 friends. How many apples will each friend receive?"

subject: mathematics
description: Repeated-subtraction problems: "John has 20 apples. He put them into bags containing 5 apples each. How many bags did John use?"

subject: mathematics
description: Use observation, questioning, worksheets and discussions to gauge students' understanding of geometric shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Students draw, build, and make predictions about 2D shapes and 3D objects in picture or in real-world context and 2D shapes undergoing a transformation

subject: mathematics
description: Play interactive games that require students to identify and name shapes, match shapes, draw, build and make predictions about 2D shapes and 3D objects

subject: mathematics
description: Play games where students identify 2D shapes undergoing a transformation

subject: mathematics
description: Game websites: splashlearn.com/math/shapes-games-for-1st-graders, abcya.com/games/shapes_geometry_game, education.com/games/first-grade/geometry/, smashmaths.com.au/measurement-and-geometry/location-and-transformation/flip-slide-and-turn

subject: mathematics
description: Play Musical Chairs and Old Grady on shapes so students' ability to identify and name shapes is highlighted

subject: mathematics
description: Mystery Bag of Shapes: Students are blindfolded, choose a shape from the mystery bag, then give a description of the chosen shape

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to draw 2D and 3D shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Create a poster on their favorite shape and its properties

subject: mathematics
description: Create a poster to advertise their favourite shape and its properties

subject: mathematics
description: Use one or more shapes to construct a compound shape. Draw a shape using a variety of plane shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to create both 2D and 3D shapes using local materials

subject: mathematics
description: Create a shape book which highlights the shapes and their properties

subject: mathematics
description: Students make a collage with different shapes and materials

subject: mathematics
description: Differentiate based on students' individual needs and abilities. Provide additional support or scaffolding for students who struggle with vocabulary. Offer extension activities for those who demonstrate proficiency

subject: mathematics
description: Tier 1: Justify the shape appropriateness for its usage in real life context

subject: mathematics
description: Tier 2: State the properties of all shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Tier 3: Classify the given shapes based on the identified attributes

subject: mathematics
description: Pupils create a mystery object using 2D shapes and 3D objects and make oral presentations

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to do show and tell on a selected shape (name the shape, properties, importance and objects it can be seen)

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they construct 3D shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they sort and group shapes according to the number of sides, corners and faces

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with a collection of 3D objects (cube, cone, sphere, cylinder). Ask them to investigate and identify different characteristics of each object (number of faces, edges, corners). Have them draw and label each object in their learning log

subject: mathematics
description: Select a 2D or 3D shape, and students should carry from home objects that reveal their shape

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students and allow students to select the most appropriate material and construct 3D shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Use rubric to assess students' oral presentations, projects, how they present their shapes in their booths, videos created that depict the properties of shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Student speaks clearly and loudly enough to be heard by the class (Always/Sometimes/Rarely)

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Student describes their 3D object using descriptive language (e.g. size, shape, color, texture, face)

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Student shares an interesting fact or story about their 3D object

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Student shows confidence and enthusiasm while presenting

subject: mathematics
description: Write about a shape they observe on their way to school

subject: mathematics
description: Have students write about what they are learning and how they feel about it: "Today I am learning about______________" "I like it because_____________"

subject: mathematics
description: Pupils create a shape log which records the different shapes that they observe in their environment for a given period

subject: mathematics
description: Provide a list of 2D and 3D shapes and students are required to match them with their corresponding names or representations

subject: mathematics
description: Use exit tickets which require students to identify the shape shown

subject: mathematics
description: Provide worksheets whereby students are required to colour each shape using a specific color code

subject: mathematics
description: Use written responses such as short answer questions or journal prompts to assess students' ability to compare the characteristics of shapes and objects

subject: mathematics
description: Use virtual manipulatives or interactive simulations to allow students to identify and name plane and solid shapes. Assess using a rubric

subject: mathematics
description: Use pictures or diagrams of everyday objects that resemble cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres. Ask students to identify and name the 2D shapes within them

subject: mathematics
description: Can be done through worksheets or digital platforms where students can drag and drop labels to match the shapes they see in the pictures

subject: mathematics
description: Use a checklist to observe students as they manipulate 2D shapes and 3D objects to describe attributes and characteristics (number of corners, sides - flat or curved)

subject: mathematics
description: Example question: "Which shape has six (6) square faces and eight (8) corners?"

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with written assignments with shapes of different size, colour and orientation that require students to differentiate shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Worksheets that require students to colour each shape using a colour code

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to match the shapes that are the same

subject: mathematics
description: Identify each shape drawn and use a colour code to colour each shape

subject: mathematics
description: Write a sentence about each shape (e.g., "The square is blue.")

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with opportunities to create drawings or diagrams of cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres. Label the features using appropriate vocabulary

subject: mathematics
description: Encourage students to identify and describe the features using appropriate vocabulary. Observe their ability to use the correct language

subject: mathematics
description: Students design their own shape using local materials and writing its attributes. Assess projects using a rubric

subject: mathematics
description: Design performance tasks to be assessed using a rubric such as building a specific 3D shape or drawing a 2D shape with a certain number of sides or corners

subject: mathematics
description: Creation of a shape book

subject: mathematics
description: Have students work collaboratively to create a shape poster

subject: mathematics
description: Have students work collaboratively to create a collage of shapes made from different materials

subject: mathematics
description: Use online formative assessment tools that provide instant feedback. Online quizzes or interactive assessments with images of 2D and 3D shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Students select the correct 2D shapes or draw on the images to highlight the shapes they identify

subject: mathematics
description: Foster a collaborative learning environment where students share their observations and ideas. Encourage students to explain their reasoning and use appropriate vocabulary (edges, vertices, sides, circles, triangles)

subject: mathematics
description: Students are given the opportunity to describe the attributes and characteristics of 2D shapes and 3D objects. Assess using a rubric

subject: mathematics
description: Students use digital drawing tools to create their own representations of 2D shapes and 3D shapes. Assess using a checklist. Sites: tuxpaint.org, sketch.io/sketchpad, google.com/intl/en_in/drive/apps.html

subject: mathematics
description: Provide a variety of real-world objects for students to describe solid shapes. Ask students to identify and name the 2D shapes they can find in given solid shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Example: a soccer ball can be used to identify a circle (for the patches) and a sphere (for the overall shape)

subject: mathematics
description: Monitor students and provide feedback on pupils' ability to transform geometric shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Take students on a shape hunt around the classroom or school. Give them a list of 2D and 3D shapes to find (circles, squares, triangles, rectangles, spheres, cubes, cylinders). Have them identify and name each shape they find

subject: mathematics
description: Ask open-ended questions that prompt students to identify and name 2D shapes. Example: "Can you point to the shape that has four sides of equal length and four right angles?"

subject: mathematics
description: Example question: "What is the name of the shape that has one curved side and no corners?"

subject: mathematics
description: Ask open-ended questions that prompt students to identify and name 3D objects. Example: "What is the name of the object that has a round base and a pointed top?"

subject: mathematics
description: Example question: "Can you name the object that has six square faces?"

subject: mathematics
description: Use games that involve identifying and naming 2D or 3D shapes: Shape memory, Shape bingo. These games make assessment more fun and engaging while allowing observation of skills

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they participate in group activities or independent work that involve sorting 2D shapes and 3D objects based on their attributes. Take note of their ability to identify and sort objects correctly

subject: mathematics
description: Ask open-ended questions that prompt students to sort 2D shapes and 3D objects based on their attributes. Example: "Can you sort these shapes based on the number of sides?"

subject: mathematics
description: Example question: "How would you sort these objects based on the number of vertices?"

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with hands-on activities such as manipulatives or shape and object models that require them to sort based on attributes. Observe their ability to use the materials to correctly sort objects

subject: mathematics
description: Use games that involve sorting 2D shapes and 3D objects based on their attributes: Attribute matching, Attribute bingo

subject: mathematics
description: Draw two overlapping circles on the board, one labeled "2D Shapes" and the other labeled "3D Objects". Ask students to name different shapes and objects and place them in the appropriate circle based on their dimensions. Encourage them to explain why they placed the shape or object in that circle

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with a set of pictures of 2D shapes and 3D objects. Ask them to sort them into two categories based on their dimensions. Can be done individually or in small groups

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they work and take notes on their progress. Identify areas where students may need additional support or guidance

subject: mathematics
description: Provide different options for creating shapes and objects (drawing, sculpting, building with blocks). Help students with different learning styles demonstrate their understanding

subject: mathematics
description: Provide hands-on activities like building with blocks or playing with 3D shapes. Observe their interactions with the objects and listen to their verbal descriptions to assess their understanding

subject: mathematics
description: Engage students in conversation about real-world objects. Ask them to describe a familiar object or use open-ended questions to prompt discussion about the properties of shapes and objects

subject: mathematics
description: Games and activities like scavenger hunts or sorting games. Example: Ask students to find objects around the classroom that are a certain shape

subject: mathematics
description: Example: Ask students to sort objects by shape

subject: mathematics
description: Ask students questions about shapes, objects, and how they are related to assess their understanding

subject: mathematics
description: Ask students to describe the shapes they see, using their own words. Allows them to use creativity and build confidence in verbal expression

subject: mathematics
description: Ask students to use manipulatives such as blocks and playdough to create geometric shapes and describe them

subject: mathematics
description: Use pictures and diagrams to help students identify different shapes. Show real-life objects and ask them to identify shapes within them

subject: mathematics
description: Use flashcards with shapes printed on them

subject: mathematics
description: Encourage students to work together in small groups to identify and describe shapes. Allows for peer-to-peer learning. Gives students opportunity to practice communication and social skills

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with individualized learning plans that cater to their specific needs. Some students may benefit from visual aids while others may need more hands-on activities

subject: mathematics
description: Use games such as shape bingo or memory matching games. Makes learning more engaging and fun for students

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students on task as they interact with different geometric shapes

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they categorize geometric shapes according to their attributes

subject: mathematics
description: Materials: Various 2D and 3D shapes (circle, square, triangle, rectangle, cube, cylinder, sphere, cone) and a large sheet of paper with two sections labeled "2D shapes" and "3D shapes"

subject: mathematics
description: Instructions: Show students the different 2D and 3D shapes and ask them to name each one. Explain that they will be sorting the shapes into two categories

subject: mathematics
description: Demonstrate how to sort by placing a circle, square, triangle, and rectangle in the "2D shapes" section, and a cube, cylinder, sphere, and cone in the "3D shapes" section

subject: mathematics
description: Have students take turns sorting shapes into the correct section. Use a timer to make it more engaging and challenging

subject: mathematics
description: Assessment: Observe students as they sort and note any difficulties. Ask students to explain why they sorted certain shapes into a particular category

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify shapes in real-world objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students name shapes correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify characteristics of each shape?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify shapes from different perspectives?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students use positional language correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students make predictions about shape transformations?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students recognize and name basic 2D shapes and 3D objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students construct shapes using appropriate materials?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students justify properties of shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students replicate and construct composite shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students compare characteristics of shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students sort and pattern shapes based on characteristics?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students describe attributes of 2D shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students describe attributes of 3D objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students compare and contrast different shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students create and extend patterns using shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students use appropriate vocabulary?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify and name 2D shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify and name 3D objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students sort shapes based on attributes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students compare and contrast 2D shapes and 3D objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students create shapes using various materials?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify real-world objects that are shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students identify basic shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students create shapes with different materials?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students find shapes in the environment?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students sort shapes based on attributes?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can students create patterns using geometric shapes?

subject: mathematics
description: Teacher will observe by listening to students as they discuss the contents of the video and engage in reproducing the pattern embedded in the video

subject: mathematics
description: Use checklist to record identified behaviors during pattern activities

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can call out the elements in the pattern in the order that they appear (left to right/right to left)

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can reproduce a pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Predict the missing elements in the pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Identify the pattern core at any point in the pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Show the part that keeps repeating in the pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Describe a pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Create a pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Observe learners as they construct their patterns. Ask questions at various cognitive levels to help learners conceptualize the characteristics of a repeating pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to learners as they respond to check for their conceptual understanding. Pay attention to their use of repeating pattern terminology

subject: mathematics
description: Use observational checklist to check and take note of misconceptions

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Selects own materials unassisted by teacher

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Selects a pattern format (ABABAB or ABCABCABC)

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Demonstrates an understanding of the pattern format with materials selected

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Explains confidently the pattern using the correct terminology

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Worked cooperatively during the activity

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Enjoyed the process in creating their product

subject: mathematics
description: As students explore the patterns, change attributes and engage in translating them in circles, they will discuss their observations and share with each other

subject: mathematics
description: Teacher will observe and record student observable behaviours using an observation checklist

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Learner can carry out the task for translating repeating patterns (Needs Intervention/Not yet approaching mastery/Approaching Mastery/Mastery)

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria - Needs Intervention: Carries out the task with assistance from the teacher

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria - Not yet approaching mastery: Carries out the task independently with 3 or more errors and/or omissions

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria - Approaching Mastery: Carries out the task independently with less than 3 errors and/or omissions

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria - Mastery: Carries out the task accurately

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Correctly translates a repeating pattern with 2 to four element core from one form to another

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric criteria: Describes/explains a pattern or pattern core/rule using the correct terminology for translating repeating patterns with 2 to 4-element cores

subject: mathematics
description: Students will have a choral singing/chanting exercise where one group will begin singing or chanting the pattern and the other group will respond/translate

subject: mathematics
description: Teacher will observe students' chants to ensure that they have understood the translation of patterns. Use the checklist to assess the translation behavior demonstrated by the learner

subject: mathematics
description: Let learners put on their "Robocop hats" before they begin the activity. Ask probing questions to help learners think about the characteristics of a repeating pattern

subject: mathematics
description: In pairs, allow students to work on spotting and correcting errors in patterns presented in different contexts. Include patterns with no errors also

subject: mathematics
description: Give learners opportunities to share their discoveries

subject: mathematics
description: Students' work will be assessed by the teacher using checklists to ensure that they use any of the pattern formats to create their designs using items found in and around the environment (ABABAB, ABCABCABC, AABAABAAB)

subject: mathematics
description: OR create their borders using the 3 shapes given, using any of the formats. Use observation checklist to assess their products

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to complete task cards to demonstrate their understanding of a repeating pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Task card example: Put a check (√) on the T-shirt with a repeating pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Task card example: Circle the pattern core

subject: mathematics
description: Task card example: Copy the pattern

subject: mathematics
description: Task card example: Circle the missing term in the pattern (multiple choice: a, b, c)

subject: mathematics
description: Learners in pairs will discuss and create their own increasing pattern to reflect body movements and share with the class their creations

subject: mathematics
description: Demonstration of the repeating pattern will be observed by the teacher and recorded for understanding using an observation chart

subject: mathematics
description: Observation chart criteria: Created and demonstrated a repeating pattern using the ABBABBABB pattern (Yes/No/Needs Practice)

subject: mathematics
description: Observation chart criteria: Presented and explained their pattern thinking process, using content vocabulary such as next, pattern, after, etc.

subject: mathematics
description: Observation chart criteria: Had fun while engaging in the pattern creation process

subject: mathematics
description: Engage learners in a discussion on what they are observing in the given sequence using coloured blocks and/or bottle caps

subject: mathematics
description: Behaviours will be recorded using an observation checklist

subject: mathematics
description: In pairs, students will then create a sequence of their own reflecting the ABABBABBB pattern and share with their peers

subject: mathematics
description: Students' progress will be monitored by the teacher as they engage in the activity

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can identify a pattern in a given sequence

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Is able to explain the pattern in the demonstrated sequence

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Is able to extend the continuing pattern within the sequence with at least 2 to 3 elements

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Is able to explain their reason for selecting the continued pattern in the given sequence

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Uses appropriate vocabulary associated with patterns (next, after, beside, sequence, pattern)

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Able to create a sequence reflecting the pattern ABABBABBB in pairs

subject: mathematics
description: As the story is read, guiding questions will be asked based on patterns observed. Example: "How many buttons do you think will be on the third shirt, 4th shirt, 5th shirt?"

subject: mathematics
description: Attention will be paid to the use of the vocabulary associated with increasing patterns (next, one more than, more)

subject: mathematics
description: Observable behaviours will be noted, such as students' ability to reproduce the pattern using different modes of representations (play dough, blocks, drawings, or any manipulative)

subject: mathematics
description: Students' ability to demonstrate their understanding of an increasing or decreasing pattern using objects of their choice will be observed

subject: mathematics
description: Students will use a self-assessment to determine how they felt about completing the task: I've got it / I am struggling but learning / I need some help

subject: mathematics
description: Self-assessment checklist criteria: I can identify the pattern in the sequence

subject: mathematics
description: Self-assessment checklist criteria: I can spot the error and say what it is

subject: mathematics
description: Self-assessment checklist criteria: I can fix the error

subject: mathematics
description: Self-assessment checklist criteria: I can extend the sequence

subject: mathematics
description: Observe and check whether learners are able to represent unknown quantities from stories or situations, using concrete materials

subject: mathematics
description: Make observations about the math material that the learners use to show the unknown quantities

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the learner write a number sentence to represent unknown quantities? (e.g., 1+ ___ = 4)

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the learner represent unknown values using concrete materials and drawings?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the learner use part-part-whole relationship to represent unknown quantities?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the learner use joining and separating to find unknown quantities?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the learner use the inverse of the operation to represent and find unknown quantities?

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners read stories/situations about unknowns. Allow them to work in pairs and discuss representations/solutions

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to make presentations using concrete materials or role play situations to represent unknowns

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners play games in small groups using "missing part cards". Use number frames (5, 10, etc.). Each card can have a different number of dots in the frames or a specific number can be the focus

subject: mathematics
description: Observe and listen as students read the cards, discuss the visible parts, represent the unknown, etc. Model the game

subject: mathematics
description: Learners can also engage in the game "I wish I had…" They will listen to, observe or read situations and then determine the representation for the unknown

subject: mathematics
description: Concrete materials or pictures can be used with the presentation of the situations

subject: mathematics
description: Distribute cards to learners and allow them to write/complete number sentences to represent unknowns from situations read to them

subject: mathematics
description: OR distribute cards and allow students to write their own stories/situations for representing unknowns and complete them

subject: mathematics
description: Collect cards or allow students to place them up in specific area in the classroom for the topic done

subject: mathematics
description: In pairs, learners create equal lengths using building blocks for given lengths

subject: mathematics
description: Monitor using activity tracking tools

subject: mathematics
description: Activity: Create lengths of building blocks that are equivalent. Skills observed: Stacked the blocks one at a time keeping in mind the previous number

subject: mathematics
description: Activity: Create lengths of building blocks that are equivalent. Skills observed: Stacked the blocks evenly as they created the equal lengths

subject: mathematics
description: Activity: Manipulate a pair of given lengths to make them equivalent to each other. Skills observed: Added blocks to the shorter length

subject: mathematics
description: Activity: Manipulate a pair of given lengths to make them equivalent to each other. Skills observed: Removed blocks from the longer length

subject: mathematics
description: Determine whether students are able to match the equal lengths on a grid. Example: D = F

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric: The learner identified three pairs of equal lengths = 3 points

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric: The learner identified 2 pairs of equal lengths = 2 points

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric: The learner identified 1 pair of equal lengths = 1 point

subject: mathematics
description: Rubric: The learner identified no equal lengths = 0 points

subject: mathematics
description: Learners are asked to create other number combinations equal to a given number combination using playing cards

subject: mathematics
description: Learners use the guess and check method to help them make equivalent playing card combinations

subject: mathematics
description: Within a minute, learners individually draw marbles to create equal combinations for the given one. In their groups, discuss and compare their responses

subject: mathematics
description: Use the remaining cards in the suit to create the other equal combinations. Share their answers with the class

subject: mathematics
description: A similar assessment can be done using dominoes

subject: mathematics
description: Learners write another combination for a given combination on a given card

subject: mathematics
description: Learners complete representations to make equivalent number combinations. Example: Draw marbles to complete the following

subject: mathematics
description: Learners compare three given number lines to identify the pair showing equal number combinations. They discuss how they can make the number combination shown on the non-example equal to the others identified

subject: mathematics
description: Learners are given a set of dominoes to find the "odd one out". The number combination on one of the dominoes will not be equivalent to the others

subject: mathematics
description: Learners state their reason for choosing the equal number combinations. They state the reason for not choosing the domino which does not belong to the set

subject: mathematics
description: Learners analyze pairs of number combinations and check true or false to state whether the pair are equal number combinations

subject: mathematics
description: Use a simple observation checklist while learners use the pan balance or arm balance to form equations

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Uses counters correctly to maintain balance (Yes/No/Sometimes)

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Correctly forms equations using counters

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Expresses equalities as different representations of the same quantity

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Formulates equations for given stories/word problems

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Creates stories/word problems for given equations

subject: mathematics
description: Note: The checklist can be modified for the use of stackable blocks, as well as 5 and 10 frames

subject: mathematics
description: Have students pull their chairs and/or desks in a physical circle in the classroom

subject: mathematics
description: The teacher calls out (or writes) three numbers and students are to formulate a number sentence using all three numbers

subject: mathematics
description: The first person to answer gets to hold a ball. This ball will be sent to the next person who answers correctly and so on

subject: mathematics
description: Have learners play fishing games in small groups to collect numbers that will make number sentences true

subject: mathematics
description: Observe learners as they discuss what is needed to make number sentences true and why

subject: mathematics
description: Allow learners to work in pairs or groups where they discuss different scenarios for given equations

subject: mathematics
description: Provide them the opportunity to present to the class using concrete materials/role play/discussion

subject: mathematics
description: In mixed ability groups, allow learners to formulate equations for given word problems/stories. This provides an opportunity for struggling learners to become more engaged (inclusion)

subject: mathematics
description: Allow them to present, by reading the problem to the class and then explaining how they arrived at the equation. Ensure that each group member plays an active role in the activity

subject: mathematics
description: Note: For presentations, a scoring rubric can be used as formative assessment

subject: mathematics
description: Ask learners to write a number sentence that equals 12

subject: mathematics
description: Each learner will be given a blank card or sticky note to write his/her number sentence. These can be posted on a board in the classroom

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student identify the pattern core?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student describe the rule of a pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student extend a pattern in both directions?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student translate a pattern from one form to another?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student spot and correct errors in a pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student create their own repeating pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student identify an increasing pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student identify a decreasing pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student explain how a pattern grows or shrinks?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student extend a growing pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student create their own increasing/decreasing pattern?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student spot errors in increasing/decreasing patterns?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student represent unknowns using concrete materials?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student write number sentences with missing values?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student use part-part-whole relationships?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student solve joining and separating problems?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student use inverse operations?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student create equal lengths/sets?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student identify equivalent number combinations?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student explain why two quantities are equal?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student use the equal sign correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student find the "odd one out"?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student write number sentences for given situations?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student use the pan balance to show equality?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student determine if equations are true or false?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student find unknown values in equations?

subject: mathematics
description: Diagnostic question: Can the student create word problems for equations?

subject: mathematics
description: Sample item: Picture of bird on tree - "The bird is _____(high, low) up on the tree."

subject: mathematics
description: Sample item: Picture of boy and tree - "The boy is _____(near/far) to/from the tree."

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to learners as they describe the characteristics of the attribute, length, of given objects. Check to see if the learner is using correct vocabulary associated with measurable attributes (length).

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the term "long" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the term "short" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the terms "near" and "far" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the terms "thick" and "thin" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the terms "narrow" and "wide" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the terms "deep" and "shallow" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: As the story "Goldilocks and the Three Bears" is read, the teacher will take note of the responses from students about the vocabulary associated with the contents in the container (full and empty).

subject: mathematics
description: Situations classifying empty and full containers in pictures will be done by students first using their self-check table in pairs then presented orally to the class.

subject: mathematics
description: In pairs, students will discuss and match the picture to the sentence that best describes it and fill in the blank space with the correct describing word.

subject: mathematics
description: Sample item: Picture of bus and building - "The bus is _____(close, far) to/from the building."

subject: mathematics
description: Sample item: Picture of baby in pool - "Babies should bathe in pools that are _____(shallow, deep)."

subject: mathematics
description: Sample item: Picture of mangoes on tree - "The mangoes can be easily reached because they are _____(low, high) on the tree."

subject: mathematics
description: To culminate the measuring attribute, length, a graphic organizer will be created with learners with all the describing words/vocabulary learnt about how long or short objects and distances are. A discussion will bring out the measuring attribute of length.

subject: mathematics
description: Teacher will observe students as they pour items into containers and group the objects as liquids that can be poured (takes up the space of the container) and items that can be poured and not liquid (not taking up all the space). Students will formalise the measuring attribute capacity from the activities.

subject: mathematics
description: Place an X on all the items that can be poured and the container has a capacity.

subject: mathematics
description: In pairs, think of objects that are heavy, objects that are light to carry around them and group them (pictures will be given as well for that discussion). Students will share with the class after classifying the objects as heavy or light.

subject: mathematics
description: All the terms associated with mass will be discussed with students and a graphic organizer will be created to assess their understanding of mass.

subject: mathematics
description: As students engage in the pizza making process, teacher will note the vocabulary associated with the area (small and large) as they discuss the activity.

subject: mathematics
description: Students will be presented with some objects to feel and touch them. They will describe what they feel (in terms of the surfaces). Teacher will listen for vocabulary such as flat surface/face, curved surface.

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to the discussions at various stations on which objects are shorter, longer and taller. Pay attention to the skills applied and the reason for selection. Make use of observational checklists.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Did the children place both objects on the same flat surface to compare their height?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify taller or shorter objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Did the student use the same start point to compare the length of the two objects?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student recognize the longer and shorter object?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student explain why objects must be aligned before they can be compared?

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with a task sheet with objects identified as longer than or shorter than other objects. Have students determine which is correct and explain why.

subject: mathematics
description: Pay attention to which objects students are trying to send the hoop around after instructions are given (e.g., "Send the hoop around the object which is nearer to you"). Focus on whether students are attempting to send the hoop around the correct object.

subject: mathematics
description: Listen as students make statements of comparison. Question students to find out their reasoning for such statements. Watch as students pick their cards to show that they have completed their task. Pay careful attention to the use of the words higher and lower in the explanations given.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe as learners glue sticks to make models of thicker, thinner, narrower and wider shelves. Provide students with the opportunity to show their work and explain by making statements of comparison.

subject: mathematics
description: Have every child show their objects to their pairs, identify which is lighter or heavier. Question students to further explain why they made such a comparison statement. Have them work in a small group to verify their answer using a hanger balance scale.

subject: mathematics
description: Have students form three groups: lighter than, heavier than, or equal in weight. After students take turns on the seesaw with their partner, they fit themselves in the right groups. After the activity, have each child state their position in comparison to their partner (e.g., "I am heavier than Joe").

subject: mathematics
description: In groups, have them select two objects - one which can be pushed or pulled and one which they can't. Provide learners with the opportunity to present objects to the class giving explanations with reasoning. Pay attention to the use of the terms heavier than and lighter than.

subject: mathematics
description: Allow students to visit different demonstration stations. Have groups explain the process they followed to determine which bottle holds more or less liquid or equal amounts of liquid. Allow students to ask questions if they need to know anything more about the process followed.

subject: mathematics
description: Pay attention to students as they compare the surface area of the books chosen. Let them discuss in pairs, as they make statements of comparison. Provide students with the opportunity to share with the class.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe pupils as they select non-standard units to measure the different attributes.

subject: mathematics
description: Make notes of students' reasoning as they discuss among themselves the difference in their answers.

subject: mathematics
description: Students use the appropriate non-standard measurement to measure items: Hands span, toy cars, foot span for length; Stone & sand for capacity; Hand span and squares for area.

subject: mathematics
description: Students estimate the non-standard measurement then measure the object. Students should justify their reasons for their estimated non-standard measurement.

subject: mathematics
description: Compare the length of an object (pencil) to the length of two or three smaller objects (eraser, sharpener).

subject: mathematics
description: Compare a surface space with two different size and shape leaves. (How many banana leaves/other leaves can cover the chalkboard? How many table mats/coasters can cover a table surface?)

subject: mathematics
description: Use blocks or tiles to build something longer than, same as, shorter than, or half as long as a given object.

subject: mathematics
description: Students work in groups or pairs to estimate measurement using non-standard units using various objects.

subject: mathematics
description: Students cover the surface of items using different sized squares or palms.

subject: mathematics
description: Students compare measurements using non-standard measurement in groups or pairs.

subject: mathematics
description: Students discuss their findings: "Why is his or her measurement more or less than yours?" "Why is there a difference in the measurements?"

subject: mathematics
description: Students use different size containers to determine which holds more.

subject: mathematics
description: Provide students with three or more objects and have them compare all three.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student select appropriate non-standard units?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student estimate before measuring?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student measure length using body parts (hand span, foot span)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student measure length using objects (paper clips, blocks)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student measure area using non-standard units?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student measure capacity using non-standard units?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student explain differences in measurements?

subject: mathematics
description: Select the correct day to complete the sentences. Online resource: https://wordwall.net/resource/22419293

subject: mathematics
description: Put the months of the year in the correct order. Online resource: https://wordwall.net/resource/363117

subject: mathematics
description: With the aid of a calendar, in small groups, students construct a story or poem utilizing the days, months, or dates in a sequential order. For example, students with the same birth month can identify each birthdate in sequential order.

subject: mathematics
description: Use a calendar to mark special days celebrated in their country.

subject: mathematics
description: Draw images to represent their favourite month of the year.

subject: mathematics
description: Complete the days of the week or months of the year starting from a given day or month (e.g., Complete by filling in the days of the week from Thursday, _____, _____, _____).

subject: mathematics
description: Students complete a worksheet in which they draw hands to show time on analog clocks.

subject: mathematics
description: Students work in pairs to tell the time using their bodies.

subject: mathematics
description: Online resources for telling time practice: https://wordwall.net/resource/24859817, https://wordwall.net/resource/32922747, https://wordwall.net/resource/54049357, https://wordwall.net/resource/363099

subject: mathematics
description: Students match a series of activities to the correct time of the day when each activity occurs. Students can justify their answers if they differ (e.g., a student may say he or she takes an afternoon nap and another may say it only happens in the evening).

subject: mathematics
description: Get students to work out how many days they have been in school using the yearly calendar. Get them to also work out how many days to other significant events such as Christmas, their birthday, or the school holidays. Students can create their own questions to swap with another student to answer.

subject: mathematics
description: Students identify the features of the different coins in their country through comparing and contrasting all the coins.

subject: mathematics
description: Sort and arrange the coins according to value.

subject: mathematics
description: Give combinations of coins that give a total value of 10c and 25c.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student state the days of the week in order?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student state the months of the year in order?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student read a date on the calendar?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify holidays on the calendar?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student tell time on the hour (analog)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student tell time at half past (analog)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student read time on a digital clock?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student match activities to time of day?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the 5c coin?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the 10c coin?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the 25c coin?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student sort coins by value?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student count coins to make a given value?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student skip count by 5s using coins?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student skip count by 10s using coins?

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to students as they formulate questions during the observation process based on the experiences provided by the teacher (nature walk, concrete objects, etc.). Check to ensure that the questions generated are related to what was observed.

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to students' answers based on the questions they construct. Check to make sure that answers supplied are related or possible. Follow-up questions can be asked to check students' understanding.

subject: mathematics
description: Ask probing questions such as: "What would you like to know about this picture/objects/people?"

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to students as they evaluate their peers as they ask questions to be used to collect data.

subject: mathematics
description: Conference students concerning their efforts at putting words together to form questions based on a situation (shapes, coins, pictures, sports). Example: "(cents many are How coins jar the five in?)"

subject: mathematics
description: Observe as students select random questions from a fish bowl; read each question, then state which data set they would use to get the information to answer the question. Non-examples should be provided to monitor students' understanding.

subject: mathematics
description: Sample questions: "How many bananas are in the bowl?" "Of all the shapes, which one has the most squares?" "Who has the least number of marbles?" "What is the gender of the tallest child?" "On which day were the most mangoes sold?"

subject: mathematics
