description: Observe students match questions to possible sources where data can be had to answer these questions; and how they justify their responses.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they work in small groups to correct questions with errors to make them clearer and/or more specific.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they interview each other based on selected scenarios/learning experiences presented. Students take turns to construct and ask questions according to the scenario presented.

subject: mathematics
description: Use a checklist to assess students as they engage in collecting and recording data. Assess their ability to record data accurately using different methods.

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to students as they work in pairs and small groups to provide justification for choice of strategies used in collecting and recording data.

subject: mathematics
description: Students answer questions to justify why they group objects the way they did.

subject: mathematics
description: Students work together to represent data collected.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they construct charts (such as tally charts, drawings, matching diagrams, arrow diagrams, etc.) to record the number of objects/people observed in selected places at a given time (people wearing different colours, vehicle colours, vehicle brands, etc.).

subject: mathematics
description: Teachers make notes of students' creativity as they mount display boards depicting the same data in an organised manner in different ways.

subject: mathematics
description: Students represent data collected relating to their birth months, daily temperature, ages of their classmates, etc. Teacher interviews students as they use concrete objects (square tiles, counters, grid papers) to construct bar charts for collected and/or given data.

subject: mathematics
description: Students present data based on different topics (birth months of students, occupations of parents, ethnicity groupings in the class/schools, tourist sightings, preferred taste in foods, preferred festivals, modes of transportation used to travel to school, etc.).

subject: mathematics
description: Example: Sort cotton balls according to the correct colour.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student formulate questions based on observation?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify sources for collecting data?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student collect data through observation?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student record data accurately?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student sort data by one attribute?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student organize data in a tally chart?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student create a pictograph?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student create a bar graph?

subject: mathematics
description: Use a checklist to assess students as they arrange items from greatest to smallest in a graph (pictograph, concrete graph, etc.).

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students working in pairs, as they reflect on the work of their peers against formulated criteria; and students give suggestions for improvements.

subject: mathematics
description: Students play a game in which they arrange themselves according to their heights (tallest to shortest).

subject: mathematics
description: Make notes of and interview students concerning the strategies they used to order members of a data set. Sample question: "How did you know you arranged yourselves correctly?"

subject: mathematics
description: Possible student responses: "We aligned ourselves in pairs first and our classmates checked to ensure that we were lined up properly" / "We picked the tallest and shortest in the group then the others fell in place in the line up."

subject: mathematics
description: Take notes of students as they use the feedback of their peers to identify improvements and adjust the quality of their work.

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to students as they justify the order for which they placed the items on their graph. Ask questions to ensure that they can distinguish between large and small numbers in data sets.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student order data from greatest to least?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the highest value in data set?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student identify the lowest value in data set?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student compare values in a data set?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student explain strategies used for ordering?

subject: mathematics
description: Listen to students; take notes of and give feedback to them as they use information from graphs, pictographs, charts and tables to answer questions.

subject: mathematics
description: Students answer questions correctly based on the information presented in bar graphs, pictographs, etc.

subject: mathematics
description: Sample questions for pictograph (Fruit Preferences): "How many children prefer apples?" "Which fruit is least preferred?" "How many more children prefer pears than bananas?"

subject: mathematics
description: Have students work in small groups to construct questions containing the vocabulary "least," "most," "more than," "less than" that can be answered using a given data set. Students from different groups exchange questions and give feedback to each other on the clarity of the questions.

subject: mathematics
description: Students demonstrate their understanding of the graph through oral presentation.

subject: mathematics
description: Make notes of students' reasoning as they match possible answers to questions based on data.

subject: mathematics
description: Students ask each other questions about the graph shown or made.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe, conference, and point the way forward as students make use of information in tables, pictographs, charts to answer questions based on a given data set.

subject: mathematics
description: Observe students as they re-represent data given in one mode into another mode of representation (e.g., pictograph to bar chart).

subject: mathematics
description: Students look at a graph provided by the teacher and make a different graph displaying the same information.

subject: mathematics
description: Discuss reasons as to what might have caused the changes in communication devices today.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student read data from graphs and charts?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student answer questions about data?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use vocabulary (most, least, more than, less than)?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student draw conclusions from data?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student make informed decisions based on data?

subject: mathematics
description: Students make informed decisions based on outcome.

subject: mathematics
description: Students are presented with a shopping scenario. They are given a certain amount of money and asked to answer particular questions: "What are you certain you can buy?" "Is it likely for you to buy two things with the amount of money you have?"

subject: mathematics
description: Show students types of transportation and ask questions: "Which type of transportation is likely to be used in your area/country?" "Which type of transportation is unlikely in your area?"

subject: mathematics
description: "Every vehicle on the road has music. What is the possibility of the next vehicle passing with music?"

subject: mathematics
description: Probability Quiz on Wordwall

subject: mathematics
description: Probability Group Sort on Wordwall

subject: mathematics
description: Probability Grade 1 game: https://wordwall.net/resource/8106684

subject: mathematics
description: Students spin the wheel and give an example of the word it lands on (maths spinner probability).

subject: mathematics
description: Students spin the wheel and state whether the sentence is "certain," "impossible," etc.

subject: mathematics
description: Students work in groups to create a chart depicting events with outcomes that are possible, impossible, and certain. They take turns to explain why the selected outcomes are placed under the headings they are placed.

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the word "impossible" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the word "possible" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student use the word "certain" correctly?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student predict outcomes of events?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student explain reasoning for predictions?

subject: mathematics
description: Checklist criteria: Can the student make informed decisions based on likelihood?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student use vocabulary to describe length (long, short, high, low, near, far)? Can the student use vocabulary to describe capacity (full, empty)? Can the student use vocabulary to describe mass (heavy, light)? Can the student use vocabulary to describe area (large, small)? Can the student classify objects based on measurable attributes?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student compare the length of two objects? Can the student compare the mass of two objects? Can the student compare the capacity of two objects? Can the student compare the area of two objects? Can the student explain reasoning for comparisons? Does the student use a common start point when comparing length?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student select appropriate non-standard units? Can the student estimate before measuring? Can the student measure using body parts? Can the student measure using objects? Can the student explain differences in measurements? Does the student understand why different people get different measurements?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student state the days of the week in order? Can the student state the months of the year in order? Can the student read a calendar? Can the student tell time on the hour? Can the student tell time at half past? Can the student identify coins and their values? Can the student combine coins to make a given value?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student formulate questions based on observation? Can the student identify what information a question will collect? Can the student identify sources for collecting data? Are the student's questions related to what was observed?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student sort objects by one attribute? Can the student explain why objects were sorted a certain way? Can the student collect data through observation? Can the student record data using tally marks? Can the student create a pictograph? Can the student create a bar graph?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student order data from greatest to least? Can the student identify the highest and lowest values? Can the student compare values in a data set? Can the student explain strategies used for ordering?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student read data from graphs and charts? Can the student answer questions about data? Can the student use vocabulary (most, least, more than, less than)? Can the student draw conclusions from data? Can the student make informed decisions based on data?

subject: mathematics
description: Can the student use "impossible," "possible," and "certain" correctly? Can the student predict outcomes of events? Can the student explain why an event is likely or unlikely? Can the student make informed decisions based on probability?
